text: The system shall allow a member of the network to send requests to a particular or all the other members about possible delivery of a driver's smart card to a similar person.
The system shall allow a member of the network to send a bulk request on all or a large part of its driver's smart card holders to a particular or all members of the network.
The system shall allow a member to perform statistics on messages issued and received from or to its system.
The system shall provide automatic reply to the sender of the request through a standard interface to the Member systems.
The system shall track the workflow between senders and related replies.
The system shall automatically transmit alert messages to senders or administrators when a constraint on delay for reply is not fulfilled.
The system shall allow the administrator to extract statistics of use, standard delay of reply by member, and percentage of unsuccessful transactions.
The system shall provide the management of user rights and permissions.
The system shall define and manage message types for pre-delivery check, stolen/lost cards, renewals, exchanges, and duplicates.
The system shall allow the inclusion of new members in the network through administrative tasks.
The system shall automate repetitive and tedious tasks to relieve the users.
The system shall provide application-level security and encryption policy including non-repudiation.
The system shall prevent any Member of the network, including the administrator, from technically reconstructing a consolidated European database.
The system shall allow a Member State to request card status from the corresponding Card Issuing Authority of the Member State having issued the card.
The system shall allow a Member State to send card status modification requests to the corresponding Card Issuing Authority of the Member State having issued the card.
The system shall allow enforcement authorities to request driver card status based on card number or driver identity data.
The system shall allow enforcement authorities to request workshop card status based on workshop card number and issuing Member State code.
The system shall guide users through an interface based on end-user concepts.
The system shall be easy to learn and not obstruct the thematic understanding of the users.
The system shall provide mechanisms to correct user mistakes easily.
The system shall be designed as a robust system that is tolerant to operator errors and recovers cleanly from power cuts or disasters.
The system shall function reliably with minimum interruptions during its first operational year.
The system shall provide stable and reproducible results.
The system shall support more than one contact point per country and work in a multi-hierarchical environment.
The system shall have no restriction in time or place for the use of the software.
The system shall establish and maintain dialogue with Member systems despite varying technical environments and technologies.
The system shall be designed so that background tasks continue while the user performs foreground tasks.
The system shall respond to user requests within 1 minute for enforcement authorities regardless of background tasks.
The system shall support different message structures to cover future networks such as driving licenses.
The system shall be maintainable and extensible.
The system shall be designed to migrate to upgraded hardware or new versions of operating systems.
The system shall be able to migrate to network types other than TESTA-II.
The system shall provide solutions for data encoding problems including different character sets and name matching rules.
The system shall be designed and documented for an operational lifetime of many years.
The system shall use the network facilities supplied by the TESTA-II network.
The system shall base software algorithms on existing techniques without requiring new research.
The system shall depend on pre-existing or commercially available software for most of its functionality.
